statement.markdown: Solve the problem and fix the bugs.
statement.inputFormat: Input is a single integer n representing the number of bits in the bitmask.
statement.outputFormat: Return true if the bitmask has lost any bits, false otherwise.

examples.explanation: The bitmask has lost the bit at position 4.
The bitmask has not lost any bits.

hints.text: Hint 1: Think about how you can use bitwise operations to check if a bit is set in a bitmask.

editorial.title: Editorial for Recover Lost Bits in Bitmask
editorial.intuition: The basic idea is to compare the given bitmask with its binary representation.
editorial.optimalSolution: You can use the fact that a bitmask with a lost bit at position i will have a different binary representation than the bitmask without the lost bit.
